title: [Claude Code] Automate PR Creation and Code Review in Your Repo
description: Use Claude Code in headless mode to automatically create pull requests and generate code review comments via the GitHub CLI.

steps.title: Install and authenticate GitHub CLI
steps.instruction: Set up the GitHub CLI (gh) so Claude Code can interact with your repository.
steps.prompt: How do I install and authenticate the GitHub CLI (gh) on my machine?
steps.expected_result: GitHub CLI installed and authenticated with your GitHub account
steps.tip: Run `gh auth status` to verify authentication

steps.title: Create a feature branch and make changes
steps.instruction: Ask Claude Code to create a new branch, make a specific improvement to your code, and commit the changes.
steps.prompt: Create a new branch called 'claude/improve-error-handling', find all places in the codebase where errors are swallowed or not properly handled, improve them, and commit the changes with a descriptive commit message.
steps.expected_result: A new git branch with committed improvements to error handling
steps.tip: Review Claude Code's changes with `git diff main..claude/improve-error-handling` before proceeding

steps.title: Create a pull request via GitHub CLI
steps.instruction: Have Claude Code create a pull request with a detailed description of the changes.
steps.prompt: Create a pull request for the 'claude/improve-error-handling' branch. The PR title should summarize the changes and the description should list: what changed, why it's better, and how to test it. Use the GitHub CLI.
steps.expected_result: A pull request created on GitHub with a clear, detailed description
steps.tip: Check the PR at github.com/your-repo/pulls after creation

challenge: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs this Claude Code automation on a weekly schedule to find and fix common code quality issues automatically.

videos.title: Claude Code GitHub Automation Tutorial
videos.search_keyword: Claude Code GitHub CLI PR automation tutorial 2026
